event: Calvary scene — right wing of triptych showing the road to Golgotha
symbols: tall cross or pole on the hilltop — Golgotha, the place of crucifixion toward which the procession moves
crowd of diverse figures — all of humanity witness to the Passion
the Virgin in blue kneeling — maternal grief and the Church mourning at the foot of the cross
hilly landscape — the Via Dolorosa climbing toward Calvary
description: A tall, narrow panel with a trefoil-shaped top, depicting a hillside scene with multiple groups of figures. At the top of the hill, two robed figures stand near a tall wooden pole or cross. Below, a crowd of men and women in varied medieval dress — including soldiers, mourners, and onlookers — ascend the green hillside. A woman in blue and white kneels or bows in the center. Figures in red robes and turbans are scattered through the composition. The background features rocky blue mountains and a dramatic sky. The scene has a processional quality, moving upward.
theological_description: This wing panel from a Calvary Triptych depicts the crowd ascending toward Golgotha, capturing the diversity of responses to the crucifixion of Christ. The mourning Virgin, the curious onlookers, the hostile soldiers — all converge on the hill where salvation history reaches its climax. The upward movement of the composition enacts the ascent to the cross, the journey that Christ undertook willingly: 'No one takes my life from me, but I lay it down of my own accord' (John 10:18). The kneeling Virgin embodies the Mater Dolorosa, the sword of Simeon's prophecy piercing her heart (Luke 2:35). The diverse crowd reflects the universality of the atonement — Christ died for all, Jew and Gentile, believer and scoffer. The cross at the summit awaits its victim, who is also the world's savior: 'And I, when I am lifted up from the earth, will draw all people to myself' (John 12:32).

scripture_refs.reason: Christ going out to Golgotha bearing his cross, the destination of this procession
When I am lifted up I will draw all people to myself — the cross as universal magnet of salvation
Simeon's prophecy of the sword piercing Mary's soul, fulfilled in her grief at Calvary
No one takes my life — Christ's voluntary journey to the cross
The women of Jerusalem weeping as Christ goes to the cross
